ci_support_linux_64_python3.10.____cpyth:
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.description:

across diverse 3D ecosystems.

name: libusd-exchange

about.description:
OpenUSD Exchange SDK accelerates OpenUSD adoption by helping developers
design, build, and test USD I/O solutions, producing consistent assets
across diverse 3D ecosystems.

OpenUSD Exchange SDK accelerates OpenUSD adoption by helping developers
design, build, and test USD I/O solutions, producing consistent assets
across diverse 3D ecosystems.

raw_meta_yaml:
context:
  version: "2.2.2"

recipe:
  name: usd-exchange-split
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/NVIDIA-Omniverse/usd-exchange/archive/refs/tags/v${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: f3937e842d288d7871577f8c9afd16c7a8e62d2a8d8cc0dff8aca2f3c3cc266e

build:
  number: 3

outputs:
  - package:
      name: libusd-exchange
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then: build_cxx.sh
        - if: win
          then: build_cxx.bat
    requirements:
      run_exports:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage('libusd-exchange', upper_bound='x.x') }}
      build:
        - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - cmake
        - doctest
        - ninja
      host:
        - if: linux
          then:
            - libgl-devel
            - libglx-devel
        - openusd
        - python
      # libgl-devel and libglx-devel is just used for CMake reasons, but
      # they are not actually linked, so we can ignore the run_exports
      ignore_run_exports:
        from_package:
          - if: linux
            then:
              - libgl-devel
              - libglx-devel
    tests:
      - package_contents:
          include:
            - usdex/core/Core.h
            - usdex/rtx/MaterialAlgo.h
          lib:
            - usdex_core
            - usdex_rtx
      - script:
          - cmake-package-check usd-exchange --targets usd-exchange::usdex-core usd-exchange::usdex-rtx
        requirements:
          run:
            - cmake-package-check
            - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
            - if: linux
              then:
                - libgl-devel
                - libglx-devel

  - package:
      name: usd-exchange
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then: build_py.sh
        - if: win
          then: build_py.bat
    requirements:
      build:
        - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - cmake
        - ninja
      host:
        - python
        - pybind11
        - openusd
        - ${{ pin_subpackage('libusd-exchange', exact=True) }}
        - if: linux
          then:
            - libgl-devel
            - libglx-devel
      run:
        - python
        - ${{ pin_subpackage('libusd-exchange', exact=True) }}
      # libgl-devel and libglx-devel is just used for CMake reasons, but
      # they are not actually linked, so we can ignore the run_exports
      ignore_run_exports:
        from_package:
          - if: linux
            then:
              - libgl-devel
              - libglx-devel
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
            - usdex.core
            - usdex.rtx
      - files:
          source:
            - CHANGELOG.md
            - source/core/tests/unittest/testCore.py
            - source/core/tests/unittest/testPxr.py
        script:
          - python -m unittest discover -v -s source/core/tests/unittest -p testCore.py
          - python -m unittest discover -v -s source/core/tests/unittest -p testPxr.py

about:
  homepage: https://github.com/NVIDIA-Omniverse/usd-exchange
  summary: OpenUSD Exchange SDK
  description: |
    OpenUSD Exchange SDK accelerates OpenUSD adoption by helping developers
    design, build, and test USD I/O solutions, producing consistent assets
    across diverse 3D ecosystems.
  license: Apache-2.0
  license_file: LICENSE.md
  repository: https://github.com/NVIDIA-Omniverse/usd-exchange

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - traversaro
  feedstock-name: usd-exchange

about.description:
OpenUSD Exchange SDK accelerates OpenUSD adoption by helping developers
design, build, and test USD I/O solutions, producing consistent assets
across diverse 3D ecosystems.
